title: LSA Audit — URLs, GMB Address Matching, and CallRail Numbers
assignees: Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, David Nickerson, Keith Bruss, Mikey King, Predrag
description:
Original Client Subject Line: Kogan LSA FW: Update to your Local Services Ads: How we're using URLs to improve ads

Domain: kogan-disalvo.com

Primary URL: Not applicable — this is an account-level audit across multiple LSA listings.

Christine is requesting a full audit of Kogan & DiSalvo's LSA account in response to a Google notification about LSAs using website URLs to generate ad content. 

The audit covers three areas: 

(1) confirming each LSA location is using the correct location-specific landing page URL, (
2) confirming each LSA address matches its corresponding GMB listing, and 
(3) confirming every LSA location uses the CallRail tracking number. One specific exception: the Boynton Beach location should use the main site URL (not a location lander) because that listing is used for statewide ads. 

Christine also flagged that the Broward County address does not match the Plantation location address and should be reviewed.

Client Goal: Ensure all LSA listings are properly configured with the correct URLs, accurate GMB-matching addresses, and CallRail tracking numbers ahead of Google's new URL-based content generation rollout.

Requirements for Completion:
  • Each LSA location is using its corresponding location-specific landing page URL, with the exception of the Boynton Beach listing, which should use the main site URL
  • The address listed on each LSA matches the address on its corresponding GMB listing (flag any mismatches, including the Broward County / Plantation discrepancy noted by the client)
  • Every LSA listing is using the CallRail tracking number
  • Every GMB location has a corresponding active LSA listing (confirm no GMB locations are missing an LSA)
  • Any discrepancies or mismatches are documented and flagged back to the AM for client communication
Deadline: No deadline stated by the client.

Assets and References:
  • Forwarded Google notification re: LSA URL-based content generation (included in client email thread)
  • Client noted the Broward County address does not match the new Plantation location — this should be a priority check item
 



 
And every LSA uses the CallRail tracking number:

 



Text from image:

"  Hello,

Local Services Ads will soon begin using the web URLs identified in your Local Services Ads account to generate content like business and service descriptions, special offers, pricing information, and discounts for your ads.

To use this feature, make sure your Local Services Ads account is updated and includes your web URL. You can confirm these settings by navigating to Account > Profile & Budget > Website.

If you don’t want us to generate new content from the website provided in your Local Services Ads account, you may remove your web URL by navigating to Account > Profile & Budget > Website.

Helpful tip 
 
Ensure your website is always current so Google can accurately share your latest service descriptions and special offers with potential customers. 
 
If you have any questions, please reach out to your account management team or contact us at any time. 
 
Sincerely,
The Local Services Ads Team  "

title: URGENT!  Broken Internal Links & Site Errors — Ahrefs Audit Ale
assignees: Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, Den, Evgenii Gusarovskii, Jordan, Keith Bruss, Predrag
description:
On May 1, 2026, an Ahrefs Site Audit alert was triggered, indicating a significant spike in critical errors on the Kogan & DiSalvo website. The site's health score dropped from 98 to 88, a decrease of 10 points. This resulted in 2,214 new errors and 2,130 internal pages linking to broken destinations. Several additional critical issues were identified, including 5XX server errors, accessibility problems with the robots.txt file, and hreflang errors. The client has forwarded the alert and is requesting confirmation once the issues are resolved. It would be beneficial to perform a quick internal check to determine if any work has been deployed on kogan-disalvo.com recently. A redirect change, page deletion, or URL restructure could explain this pattern.

Client Goal: Identify the source of the sudden spike in critical site errors and resolve all broken internal links and server errors affecting the site.

Requirements for Completion:
  • Root cause of the sudden spike in errors is identified
  • Internal pages linking to broken destinations are corrected or redirected appropriately
  • 5XX server errors are resolved and no longer appearing
  • Robots.txt is accessible and functioning correctly
  • Hreflang errors (broken/redirect targets, missing reciprocal tags, non-canonical targets) are corrected
  • 3XX redirects in sitemap are cleaned up
  • Site health score reflects improvement in next crawl
  • AM is notified when resolved so client can be updated

Deadline: No hard deadline stated, but this is an active technical issue with SEO implications at scale. Treat as urgent.

Assets and References:
  • Ahrefs Site Audit alert forwarded by Christine Stewart (cstewart@koganinjurylaw.com), Director of Marketing
  • Ahrefs crawl date: May 1, 2026
  • Full issue breakdown available in Ahrefs under project "Kogan-disalvo"

Kevin Major Jr. is a dedicated personal injury litigation attorney in the state of Florida, specializing in representing clients injured in car accidents, slip and fall incidents, and other matters involving premises liability. A native of South Florida, he is committed to advocating justice and fair compensation on behalf of his clients who have been victims of the negligent actions of others. Mr. Major’s previous experience as a defense attorney for insurance companies provides him with valuable insights into opposing strategies, allowing him to better position his clients for favorable outcomes. With extensive litigation experience, he is equipped to handle cases through all stages, from intake to trial preparation and representation, and the final verdict to pursue maximum compensation for the injured. 

Mr. Major earned his Juris Doctor degree from the University of Florida Levin College of Law and holds undergraduate degrees in political science and economics from the University of Central Florida. He is an active member of the Orange County Bar Association, the Florida Bar – Young Lawyers Section, and the Paul C. Perkins Bar Association. For more than 40 years, the Paul C. Perkins Bar Association has been the professional association that is dedicated towards the advancement of African American in the legal profession within the Central Florida African American community. 

He has also been nominated as a Best Lawyers Ones to Watch from 2024 through 2026, an award only given to attorneys who are early in their careers, 10 years or less in the field, and have shown a successful professional experience. His genuine dedication to justice drives his efforts against large corporations and insurance companies, establishing a strong record of results and earning recognition as a trusted advocate during some of the most challenging periods in his clients’ lives.




__________________

Thanks!

title: New Email from Christine: Keyword Evaluation
description:
, I thought you might want to have eyes on this email from Christine.

She's asking for a comparison of historical keyword data, contrasting Q1 2024 (when we started with her) with where we are today. She's specifically asked for visibility into how their organic positions and local pack ranking have changed before and after Kogan & DiSalvo signed on with Clixsy.

Here's her specific request, it came through at 6:53 AM today:

Hi Clayton and team,
 
I wanted to have you review some historical keyword data that shows rankings form Q1 2024 compared to what they are today. I would like to compare our organic positions and Local Pack Ranking for Boca Raton from before Clixsy took over to where we are today, specifically the Boca Raton area.
 
Could you please check the organic positions today against the Q1 2024 rankings for the following keywords:
Boca Raton – Car Accident Related
  • boca raton car accident lawyer
  • car accident lawyer boca raton
  • boca car accident lawyer
  • car accident attorney boca raton
  • car accident lawyer boca
  • boca raton car accident attorneys
  • boca raton car accident attorney
  • boca car accident attorneys
Boca Raton – Personal Injury Related
  • boca raton personal injury lawyer
  • personal injury lawyer boca raton
  • boca raton personal injury attorneys
  • personal injury attorney boca raton
  • personal injury lawyer boca
  • personal injury attorneys boca raton
  • boca raton personal injury attorney
Other Related High-Value Terms
  • pedestrian accident attorneys boca raton
  • boca raton pedestrian accident lawyer
  • boca raton medical malpractice attorney
  • workers compensation attorney boca raton
  • slip and fall attorney boca raton
 
If possible, I’d appreciate it if you could:
  1. Provide Q1 2024 positions by keyword.
  2. Provide today’s rankings for these keywords.
  3. Provide CTR from Q1 2024 vs. today.
  4. Share any insights into what may have contributed to changes in rankings and the 3rd party program you used to pull the data.
I'm not sure if this is a request we can or want to fulfill. Since you're so high-touch with her, I thought I'd send it your way for you to evaluate next steps.

Thank you!

description:

2.  Replace "Our Community" with "Our Commitment to Florida Communities" and update the content to say:

At Kogan & DiSalvo, serving our communities is central to who we are. We support programs that strengthen families, empower students, uplift underserved groups, and celebrate the individuals and organizations that make Florida a better place to live. Our involvement reflects our mission:
We Care. We Prepare. We Fight to Win.


3. Please remove old events and add new ones below:
- for now they will be regular blocks no pages to go to

 
Health & Wellness Initiatives
We proudly support local 5K events and wellness programs that raise awareness for cancer prevention, family services, and children’s health. Our team participates in and sponsors community runs and walks that bring families together and highlight important causes in our region.
Examples include:
  • Participation in community breast cancer and family health 5K events.
  • Support for wellness-based fundraisers benefiting local hospitals and cancer institutes.
 
Youth & Education Support
We invest in programs that help students, teachers, and school communities thrive. From contributing to school supply drives to supporting academic and extracurricular programming, we aim to create opportunities that encourage learning and growth.
Examples include:
  • Contributions to local school supply drives organized by legal and civic associations.
  • Sponsorship of school programs that enhance student activities and campus initiatives.
  • Support for university-driven innovation and community programming.
 
Local Nonprofit Partnerships
We collaborate with nonprofits that provide essential services to seniors, families, and underserved populations. These partnerships allow us to help strengthen the safety net for those who need it most.
Examples include:
  • Year-round support for social service organizations providing food, care, and family assistance.
  • Participation in volunteer programs and drives that support local families.
 
Community Media Partnerships
Through long-standing media relationships, we help highlight the stories that matter most—local families, educators, and student athletes making a positive impact in our community.
Examples include:
  • Weekly segments celebrating exceptional teachers and coaches.
  • Features spotlighting adoptive families and children seeking permanent homes.
  • Community-focused coverage supporting youth athletics and local schools.
 
Animal Welfare & Shelter Support
We believe every pet deserves a safe, loving home. Our team supports local animal shelters with donations, care packages, and initiatives that help animals transition into their new families.
Examples include:
  • Donation of adoption care baskets for new pet owners.
  • Support for shelter programs that assist animals in need.
 
Youth Athletics & Mentorship
Positive outlets and strong mentors make a meaningful difference in a young person’s life. We support youth athletic programs that build leadership, teamwork, and confidence.
Examples include:
  • Sponsorship of local athletic leagues and youth mentorship initiatives.
  • Support for programs that offer safe, structured activities for kids and teens.
 
Business & Community Leadership
We remain actively involved with chambers, professional groups, and civic organizations that help strengthen South Florida communities and local business environments.
Examples include:
  • Engagement with regional business associations.
  • Participation in programs that support local professionals and community leadership.”

title: Add new attorney - Casey Gartland
description:
Seeking help their new attorney on their site please. 
https://www.kogan-disalvo.com/our-attorneys/

Name: Casey Gartland 
Position: Attorney

Bio:
Mr. Gartland is a proud graduate of Port Charlotte High School, he went on to earn his Bachelor of Science in Criminal Forensic Studies and Criminal Justice from Florida Gulf Coast University, graduating with honors, followed by a Master of Science in Criminal Justice from the University of Cincinnati. He later earned his Juris Doctor from the University of Florida Levin College of Law, where he was active in trial team, served as president of the Law Student Transfer Organization, and received multiple distinctions for advocacy, pro bono work, and academic focus in criminal justice.

With nearly fifteen years of experience representing injured individuals across Florida, Mr. Gartland joined Kogan & DiSalvo in late 2025. He is known for his compassionate, client-centered approach—prioritizing each client’s medical recovery and long-term well-being before pursuing resolution. His heartfelt commitment to justice fuels his fight against large corporations and insurance companies, earning him a proven track record of results and recognition as a trusted advocate during some of the most difficult moments in his clients’ lives.

The legal community has consistently recognized Mr. Gartland’s dedication and achievements. He has been named to The National Trial Lawyers “Top 40 Under 40” list every year from 2021 to 2025 and honored as a Super Lawyers “Rising Star” from 2023 to 2025—an accolade awarded to less than 2.5% of attorneys in Florida. He has also served on the executive board and as President of the Lee County Bar Association Young Lawyers Division, as well as on the board of directors of the Lee County Bar Association Foundation. Outside the courtroom, he gives back through community service, philanthropy, and enjoys time with family, outdoor activities, music, and the beaches of Southwest Florida.



Thanks much!

title: Add Google Support Tag Implementation Appointment
description:
Hi,

We got an email from the Account Manager that they received and email from Kogan's PPC vendor. They say they "need help installing some code for enhanced conversion tracking with Google."

They say they need this done by July 11th. If there is still anything needed from the client please let us know.

Below us the email they sent. I have attached the screenshot and the text version I was able to copy from it.

 

Hello Team,

Your case 8-9528000039100 is currently awaiting additional input in order to proceed.

Thank you for your time during our recent conversation regarding the implementation of Ads Enhanced Conversions on your lead form. To ensure we're aligned and to provide clear next steps, here's a summary of what we discussed and the actions we've taken:

Summary of Actions Taken:
  • Reviewed the lead form, enabled Enhanced Conversions in the Google Ads account, and configured Google Tag Manager using the automatic collection method.
  • During testing, the setup picked up the email from the landing (thank you) page, which was the website’s own email and not the user’s input.
  • Excluded the incorrect selector and retested, but it continued to pick up content from the landing page.
  • Recommended proceeding with manual implementation for better accuracy and consistency.
  • Shared the manual implementation code so it can be passed to the web developer.

Next Steps and Required Inputs:
To proceed effectively, we require the following information/actions from you:

[Required Input ]:
Please forward this email to your web developer(s) so that the required code can be deployed at the backend appropriately. I had a check on the forms that you'd like to set up Enhanced Conversions for, and I see that the customer's email address is not passed on to the Thank You page.

At least one of the following fields must be provided for enhanced conversions to work properly:
  • Email (preferred)
  • [Optional] A phone number can also be provided along with an email or full name and address (but not by itself)

The best way to pass it is to keep it hidden in the View Source or Elements in a Variable or pass it through a dataLayer with an Event. To help you understand the schema of dataLayer code, please refer  to this code. To pass the dataLayer code for Enhanced Conversions via Google Tag Manager method, please refer to  this support article.

Please let us know once this step is completed by your web developer(s), so we can schedule another appointment to validate the implementation process.
Deadline: 11th June

Timeline and Expectations:
Once we receive the requested information, we will proceed with reviewing the updated implementation and validate the Enhanced Conversions setup together on a scheduled call.
We anticipate completing this process as soon as we have an update from you.

Additional Notes:
If you or your web developer(s) have any questions or need clarification on the code or setup process, please feel free to reach out. I will be happy to assist and prioritize your query.

Please review this summary and provide the requested information by the deadlines mentioned above.

If you have any questions or require further clarification, please reply to this email at the earliest opportunity so that we can complete your Enhanced Conversions implementation for https://www.kogan-disalvo.com/.

Thanks!

title: Update Privacy Policy...
description:
https://www.kogan-disalvo.com/privacy-policy/

SMS Terms & Conditions:
By subscribing to Kogan & DiSalvo SMS services, you agree to receive text messages from us. Kogan & DiSalvo will send SMS messages to update customers on project statuses and enhance the overall customer experience. For assistance, please contact us at (561) 375-9500. To opt out, reply "STOP" at any time. Message frequency may vary, and standard message and data rates may apply.

Text/SMS Marketing:
If you opt into SMS communications from Kogan & DiSalvo you will receive text messages at the number provided. These may include promotional offers, coupons, and updates related to Kogan & DiSalvo or our affiliated businesses, along with order and account information.

Consent to Receive Automated Messages:
Your consent to receive automated messages is not a condition for purchasing or using Kogan & DiSalvo services. Messages may include order confirmations, updates, and special offers.

Frequency of Messages:
Message frequency may vary based on orders, account activity, and promotions. Messages will include transactional updates and occasional promotional content.

Opt-Out and Support Options:
To stop receiving text messages, text "STOP" at any time. Once your opt-out request is processed, you will no longer receive messages from us. A confirmation message will be sent upon unsubscribing. To re-subscribe, text "START."
For assistance, text "HELP" or contact us at 561-375-9500.

Wireless Carrier Rates and Services:
Standard message and data rates may apply. Wireless carriers are not responsible for delayed or undelivered messages.

Confidentiality:
We will keep your mobile number confidential and will not share it with third parties for marketing purposes. However, for operational purposes, your name and number may be shared with service providers who facilitate SMS communications. These providers will use your information solely for delivering these services.
No mobile information will be shared with third parties for marketing/promotional purposes. The privacy policy excludes text messaging originator opt-in data and consent, which will not be shared with third parties. Users can opt out by replying "STOP" or request more information by replying "HELP." Message frequency may vary, and standard rates may apply.
---

title: New Blog Post w/ YouTube Embed
description:
Hi, ! We have a new request from Christine for a blog post. She'd like us to embed this YouTube video:

https://youtu.be/-H0vaTP90cc?si=K5-JoX8jfcN-nk82

And here's the copy:

Advocating for Accountability in High-Profile Brightline Lawsuits
When a tragic incident makes headlines, the stakes are high, and the legal landscape becomes more complex. At Kogan & DiSalvo, we are proud to take on highly publicized cases and large-scale lawsuits, ensuring that justice is served and meaningful changes are pursued. Recently, we have been entrusted with representing the family of James "Jim" Ostrowski, a beloved Boca Raton resident, in their wrongful death lawsuit against Brightline Trains Florida, LLC—a case that has drawn significant media attention and public concern.
Fighting for Justice in the Face of Tragedy
The Ostrowski family’s case against Brightline centers on the tragic death of Jim Ostrowski, a retired executive, community leader, and family man. On October 25, 2022, Mr. Ostrowski was fatally struck by a Brightline train at a railway crossing in a "quiet zone" near SW 18th Street and South Dixie Highway. The lawsuit alleges negligence on Brightline’s part, citing inadequate safety signage, visibility issues, and a history of safety concerns.
Key points from the case include:
  • Faded and Non-Compliant Safety Signage: The "No Train Horn" sign was reportedly faded and illegible, failing to meet federal safety standards.
  • Obstructed Visibility: Dense foliage and a curvature of the track made it difficult for pedestrians to spot approaching trains.
  • Pattern of Danger: Brightline is known as the deadliest train per mile in the U.S., with numerous fatalities since its inception in 2017.
As stated by Attorney Todd Baker of Kogan & DiSalvo, “This lawsuit isn’t just about Jim—it’s about ensuring Brightline adopts real and lasting safety measures to protect the public.”
Why Kogan & DiSalvo Stands Out in High-Profile Cases
Handling a case of this magnitude requires not only legal expertise but also the ability to navigate the intense public and media scrutiny that comes with it. Kogan & DiSalvo is uniquely equipped to take on such challenges, offering:
  • Compassionate Client Advocacy: We understand the profound emotional toll these cases have on families and work tirelessly to honor their loved ones’ memories.
  • Proven Legal Strategies: With a track record of success in complex personal injury and wrongful death cases, we approach every claim with meticulous attention to detail.
  • Commitment to Accountability: Our goal extends beyond financial compensation; we strive for systemic change to prevent future tragedies.
In the Ostrowski case, this means advocating for stronger safety protocols at railway crossings, ensuring compliance with federal standards, and holding Brightline accountable for its operations.
Making a Difference Beyond the Courtroom
For the Ostrowski family, the lawsuit is about more than seeking justice—it’s about creating safer communities. As Mrs. Ostrowski, Jim’s wife of 52 years, shared, “This tragic and preventable loss has deeply affected our family. We hope to hold Brightline accountable and push for critical safety reforms.”
This sentiment underscores the impact high-profile cases can have on public safety and corporate accountability. At Kogan & DiSalvo, we take pride in standing beside families like the Ostrowskis, ensuring their voices are heard and their efforts lead to meaningful change.
Trust Kogan & DiSalvo for Complex, High-Stakes Cases
When you choose Kogan & DiSalvo, you’re partnering with a team that doesn’t shy away from challenges. Whether it’s a high-profile lawsuit or a sensitive personal injury case, we bring the same dedication, expertise, and compassion to every client.
If you or a loved one has experienced a tragic loss or injury due to negligence, reach out to us today. Together, we can pursue justice and work toward a safer future.
At Kogan & DiSalvo, we don’t just handle cases—we advocate for change. Let us fight for you.



Thank you!!

title: Add Brigitte Jolliet to the Spanish and English sites
description:
We have a new attorney to add to the site! Here's her headshot and bio:



English

Brigitte Jolliet Bio

Brigitte Jolliet is a litigation attorney at Kogan & DiSalvo, P.A., where she specializes in
personal injury law. Ms. Jolliet is dedicated to advocating for accident victims across
Florida, leveraging her expertise to secure justice and positive outcomes for her clients.
Ms. Jolliet’s journey in law began with a solid educational foundation. A South Florida
native, she graduated magna cum laude from the Honors College at Florida
International University and gained practical experience as a personal injury case
manager. She went on to earn her Juris Doctor from Georgetown University Law
Center, where she graduated as a Blume Public Interest Scholar.

Ms. Jolliet’s commitment to advocacy extends beyond her academic achievements.
Before law school, she served as a Survivor Advocate at Kristi House Children’s
Advocacy Center, where she supported youth victims of exploitation and worked closely
with the Miami Children’s Courthouse child trafficking division. For her impactful work in
child advocacy, she received the 2020 Champion for Children Award from the Child
Welfare League of America.

During her time in law school, Ms. Jolliet further honed her public service skills by
clerking with the U.S. Senate Small Business Committee and interning for U.S. Senator
Marco Rubio. After graduation, she joined Senator Rubio’s office as a legal policy
advisor, working directly with constituents on issues ranging from criminal justice reform
to constitutional law.

Ms. Jolliet also brings valuable policy experience from her role as a legal policy analyst
with the National Governors Association, where she co-led federal grant initiatives
focused on juvenile justice reform and criminal justice deflection programs.
Bringing her diverse background in legal analysis and advocacy to Kogan & DiSalvo,
Ms. Jolliet is passionate about helping clients navigate the complexities of personal
injury cases and making a meaningful impact on her community.

Spanish

Biografía de Brigitte Jolliet

Brigitte Jolliet es abogada litigante en Kogan & DiSalvo, P.A., donde se especializa en
derecho de lesiones personales. La Sra. Jolliet se dedica a defender a las víctimas de
accidentes en todo el estado de Florida, utilizando su experiencia para obtener justicia
y resultados positivos para sus clientes.

El camino de la Sra. Jolliet en el derecho comenzó con una base educativa sólida.
Nacida en el sur de Florida, se graduó magna cum laude en el Honors College de la
Universidad Internacional de Florida y adquirió experiencia práctica como gestora de
casos de lesiones personales. Ella pasó a obtener su Juris Doctor de la Universidad de
Georgetown Law Center, donde se graduó como un Blume Public Interest Scholar.

El empeño de la Sra. Jolliet en la defensa de los derechos va más allá de sus logros
académicos. Antes de ingresar en la facultad de Derecho, trabajó como defensora de
sobrevivientes en Kristi House Children's Advocacy Center, donde prestó apoyo a
jóvenes víctimas de explotación y trabajó junto con la división de tráfico de menores del
Tribunal de Menores de Miami. Por su impactante trabajo en la defensa de los niños,
recibió el Premio Campeón 2020 para los Niños de la Liga de Bienestar Infantil de
América.

Durante sus estudios en la Facultad de Derecho, la Sra. Jolliet perfeccionó sus
aptitudes para el servicio público trabajando como secretaria en el Comité de Pequeñas
Empresas del Senado de los Estados Unidos y realizando prácticas para el senador
Marco Rubio. Después de graduarse, se unió a la oficina del senador Rubio como
asesora de política jurídica, trabajando directamente con los electores en cuestiones
que van desde la reforma de la justicia penal hasta el derecho constitucional.

La Sra. Jolliet también aporta una valiosa experiencia política de su papel como
analista de política legal con la Asociación Nacional de Gobernadores, donde co-dirigió
iniciativas de subvenciones federales centradas en la reforma de la justicia juvenil y los
programas de desviación de la justicia penal.

La Sra. Jolliet aporta a Kogan & DiSalvo su amplia experiencia en el análisis y la
defensa jurídica, y le apasiona ayudar a los clientes a navegar por las complejidades de
los casos de lesiones personales y tener un impacto significativo en su comunidad.
Let me know if you have any questions! Thank you!

title: Blog correction
description:
Relevant page: https://www.kogan-disalvo.com/car-accident/what-factors-can-affect-the-value-of-my-car-accident-claim/

The laws regarding liability in Florida have recently changed. Please replace the following text:

If liability in a car accident is clear, the non-at-fault driver’s settlement amount will be higher. However, if liability is unclear, compensation may not come or may be lower than expected.

Compensation becomes more complicated if both parties are found partially responsible for the car accident. Here are some examples of what may happen if both parties are responsible for an accident:

  • In contributory negligence states, if a driver is found even 1% at fault for an accident, they are not entitled to compensation from the other driver.
  • In pure comparative negligence states, if a driver is 75% at fault, they can still get 25% compensation from the other driver for injuries.
  • In modified comparative negligence states, a driver can file a compensation claim as long as they are not 50% or 51% responsible for the accident. 
Florida is a pure comparative negligence state, so drivers can get compensation for an accident regardless of who is at fault.

With this:

Florida is a contributory negligence state. That means if you, the injured victim, are more than 50% liable (responsible) for causing the accident,  you will recover nothing from the defendant or their insurance company. If you are less than 50% at fault, you are able to recover your damages (reduced by any percentage of liability (responsibility) attributed to you.


do we want to expand on the replacement text for SEO purposes, or are you content with this swap?

title: Case Results Website Upgrade Request
description:
Context: Kogan & DiSalvo has submitted a detailed strategic brief requesting a full redesign and expansion of their Case Results section as part of their "Over $1 Billion Recovered" brand initiative. The request involves two distinct pieces: 

  1. Redesigned the Case Results cards section
  2. Dedicated SEO-optimized landing pages for individual case results.  Case results sheet

The client is also requesting an AI-assisted content generation workflow, a custom WordPress post type system with structured custom fields and taxonomies, schema markup implementation, and a full development scope and timeline estimate. No scope has been approved yet — the client is asking us to come back with recommendations first.


Key Questions / Clarifications Needed:


 
  • I created a layout concept for this section and page by duplicating a page from the Kogan website to assist with this request. https://docs.google.com/document/d/1SiZnRaVe1yZjs82CbI2s0ShwphbONDLMnLPsWl_SluQ/edit?usp=sharing
  • Is a Litify + Zapier integration something we can evaluate or support, or is that out of scope entirely?
  • What is our recommended approach for the AI-assisted draft generation workflow feeding into WordPress — is this something we can build or facilitate?
  • What does a realistic timeline look like for the development components?

  • What is the recommended URL structure and taxonomy setup for Case Results pages from an SEO standpoint?
  • Which schema types are recommended and realistic to implement (FAQ, Article, LegalService, Attorney, Breadcrumb)?
  • How should internal linking be structured across case result pages, practice areas, and office location pages?
  • Are there SEO risks or considerations with AI-generated draft content at this scale we should flag to the client?
  • What does a strong month-ahead SEO recommendation look like for this initiative?

  

  • Does this request require a change order conversation before we respond to the client?
  • How should we frame our response to the client — are we presenting a formal proposal, a scoping call, or a written recommendation?
  • Is there a timeline expectation on the client's side we need to address?

Relevant Client Message:

"Please come back with: Recommended WordPress structure, Recommended custom fields and taxonomy setup, Recommended AI content generation workflow, Recommended review / approval workflow, SEO recommendations, Development scope, Estimated timeline."

Relevant links:

https://www.kogan-disalvo.com/

title: GA4 Attribution Issue
assignees: Barnes, Keith Bruss, Matthew Nyahari, Predrag
description:
Seeking help check these on the site please.


The client reported an issue on their end and would like to get the following checked please. So far I think they are related to the site but if some are for SEO please let me know I can create a separate task for them.


Their goal is to identify the root cause of the 2,217 "not set" visits in GA4 and resolve any tracking, tagging, or attribution issues so traffic sources are accurately reported.


Requirements for Completion:

CallRail / Phone Tracking:

  • Confirm whether CallRail or phone tracking events are being sent to GA4 with proper attribution context, including client ID, session ID, landing page, source, medium, campaign, and referrer


Chat / Popup / Widget Events:

  • Confirm whether chat, popup, or widget events (PopupYes, WidgetOpen) are being counted as key events or conversions when they may only represent engagement rather than actual leads


Backend Event Integrations:

  • Confirm whether any events are being sent through GTM, server-side tracking, Zapier, CallRail, or another backend integration without page_location, session_id, or campaign/source data


GA4 Tag Sequencing:

  • Confirm whether the GA4 config tag is firing before all event tags — attribution can be disrupted if event commands fire before the GA4 config command
  • Confirm whether the session_start event is firing correctly


Consent Mode / Cookie Banner:

  • Confirm whether any Consent Mode or cookie banner settings are preventing source/campaign information from being passed across pages


Google Ads Linking:

  • Confirm whether Google Ads is properly linked to GA4 and auto-tagging is enabled with no redirects stripping the gclid


UTM Consistency:

  • Confirm whether manually tagged URLs have complete and consistent UTMs, especially for Simpli.fi, paid social, email, sponsorships, QR codes, or other non-Google campaigns


Event Names Flagged by Client for Review:

  • repeat_phone_call
  • PopupYes
  • WidgetOpen
  • user_engagement
  • page_view
  • session_start

Thanks much!

"I need to send this to you, Christine, and Darryl so that you guys can analyze it."

He also mentioned at [20:25](https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KNAP4T73Y7WQE2379EMFG7AY?t=1225):

So Clayton committed to sending the brand bible document to Christine Stewart and Darryl Kogan for their review and analysis, with the expectation that they would provide feedback on the brand identity, differentiators, values, and the proposed tagline "We prepare, we fight to win."

title: Coral Gables Location – Phone Number Confirmation
description:
Hi Mikey,

After confirming with Christine that we created the Coral Gables location page, she came back with this email:



In this email, Christine asked us to verify the phone numbers and provided an additional number she had on file, which appears to be a CallRail number.

The phone number to use on the website is 305-424-2827. This is the number Christine originally provided, and it has already been added to the location page. The other number, 305-676-8001, is a call tracking number.

From our previous discussions (in a Gould task), it should not be used as the primary contact number. When we create the Google Business Profile after June 1st, we need to ensure that 305-424-2827 is used there as well to maintain consistency. Can you please confirm that my understanding is accurate before we close out this task?

Below is the email I would like to send to Christine for your approval:


Hi Christine,

Thank you for sending over the details for the Coral Gables location!

We wanted to confirm that the phone number we have on the website for that location is 305-424-2827, which is the NAP number you provided. When it comes time to create the Google Business Profile after June 1st, we'll make sure that same number is used there as well. This ensures everything stays consistent across the website and Google, which is important for local search performance.

The other number you referenced (305-676-8001) appears to be a call tracking number, so we'll keep that separate and not use it as the main listed number.

Please let us know if you have any questions or if anything needs to be updated!

Best,

title: CallRail External Form Tracking info
assignees: Barnes, Corey V, Keith Bruss, Matthew Nyahari
description:
Hi Corey and Keith,

Opening up this discussion task for help. We got a ticket from their AM for advise that we can provide to the client about her query. Seeking help if you can please discuss the following info below: 
  • Assess what's involved. Is this a separate scope/budget for consulting, or do we find an alternative, such as enabling Micronet to have the level of access they need to make those determinations about feasibility
  •  If this is something we could assess within a reasonable time frame, like, say, a couple of hours of assessment. 

Context: Christine from Kogan wants to explore Call Rails' external form tracking to capture conversions across the funnel, not just surface level. 

She would like us to assess and advise on the following:
  1. Feasibility
    • Whether our current site architecture and form providers support CallRail external form tracking.
  2. Full-Funnel Data Capture
    • Whether this setup would allow us to reliably pass submission data (more info than we currently pass) into CallRail and then through to Litify (and multi-touch attribution data into Litify and align timestamps/IDs for OTT) to better connect:
      • Ad source → form submission → intake → signed case.
  3. Implementation Requirements
    • Any required JavaScript, dataLayer updates, GTM configuration, or form-level changes.
  4. Testing & Rollout Plan
    • Estimated timeline and level of effort for implementation and validation.
 
If ever we need further info please do let us know we can seek further help from their AM.

Thanks so much!

CC

title: BOT Contact Form Submission
description:
Hi Clayton and Dorin,

Seeking help regarding a ticket that was raised to us. I have included the ticket verbatim below:
---
The Kogan team has noticed quite a few form submissions coming from a link on learnarticle.com, which is directing users to our motorcycle accidents page. They've been hoping this issue would resolve itself, but it’s been almost two weeks now.

title: Immediate Fixes & Clarifications Needed
description:
Seeking help with a client ticket below. I have included the email info to be sure.

The Kogan & Disalva team conducted an Ahrefs audit and identified several critical SEO issues that require attention in order to protect rankings and enhance crawlability. They would like us to prioritize these areas and provide a proposed timeline for resolution.


High-Priority Fixes
  • Sitemap & Indexation
    • ~996 indexable pages missing from the sitemap
    • 164+ pages marked “noindex” (158 noindex/follow, 6 noindex/nofollow)
    • 4 canonicals pointing to redirects
    • 13 orphan pages
    → Question: Is there a reason these pages were left off the sitemap or marked noindex, or were they overlooked?
  • Broken Links & Redirects
    • 1,254 internal links to broken pages (plus 182 on non-indexable pages)
    • 11 broken redirects, 6 redirect chains
    → These are likely diluting authority — can we prioritize fixing the highest-value pages first?
  • Content / Metadata
    • 463 cases where page titles and SERP titles do not match
    • 11 missing meta descriptions (plus 42 on non-indexable pages)
    • H1 issues: 1 missing, 7 missing on non-indexable pages, 2 pages with multiple H1s
    → What’s the best approach to aligning titles — should we standardize around SERP titles, or revise to match on-page?
  • Images & Performance
    • 1,440 images missing alt text
    • 10 images flagged as too large
    • 13 slow-loading pages
    → Can we bulk-optimize alt tags programmatically, or will this require manual tagging?
  • Structured Data
    • 165 schema validation errors
    → Do you see a common cause here (e.g., template issue), or are they spread across different content types?
  • Traffic Signals
    • 61 pages with organic traffic drops
    • 42 pages dropped out of the Top 10
    → Can you cross-reference these with the technical issues above to see if there’s correlation?

If we need more info please let us know we can seek help from their AM.

Thanks much.

CC

Should we, maybe you could tell us where we should be putting our office based on competition, where we'll have the quickest shot at, basically where it's not as oversaturated with lawyers. Now, the other part of that equation is, though, one of the reasons lawyers might not be in an area is because it may be a high crime, bad neighborhood to be in. title: CTR Optimization for Top 20 Pages
description:
Checklist: 
  •  Pull top 20 pages by impressions (GSC) 
  •  Rewrite meta using: 
    •  Primary keyword 
    •  Location (if relevant) 
    •  Benefit / hook 
Example Format:
“Miami Car Accident Lawyer – No Win, No Fee”

Success Metric:
  •  CTR increase within 2–3 weeks

title: Remove Redirect Chains & Clean Internal Links
description:
Checklist: 
  •  Identify redirect chains (Screaming Frog) 
  •  Update internal links to final destination URLs 
  •  Ensure only 1-hop redirects max
Impact: Faster crawl + improved PageRank flow

title: Fix All 4xx Errors
description:
Checklist: 
  •  Export all 4xx URLs from crawl 
  •  Map each to: 
    •  Relevant live page (301 redirect), OR 
    •  Restore if valuable 
  •  Update internal links pointing to broken URLs 
Success Metric:
 
  •  0 internal links to 4xx pages 
  •  All 4xx URLs resolved or redirected

title: Issue with translation plugin
assignees: Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, Mary De Guzman, Predrag
description:
We have an issue with the translation plugin.

It translates URLs differently, creating 404 pages. Here is an example:

https://www.kogan-disalvo.com/es/boca-raton-lesion/abogado-accidentes-auto/beneficio-de-legal-representacion/
https://www.kogan-disalvo.com/es/boca-raton-lesion/abogado-accidentes-auto/conduccion-distraida/
https://www.kogan-disalvo.com/es/boca-raton-lesion/abogado-accidentes-auto/cuanto-tiempo-tomara-mi-florida-coche-accidente-indemnizacion-tomar/
https://www.kogan-disalvo.com/es/boca-raton-lesion/abogado-accidentes-auto/evidencia/
https://www.kogan-disalvo.com/es/boca-raton-lesion/abogado-accidentes-auto/herido-borracho-conductor-reclamaciones/
https://www.kogan-disalvo.com/es/boca-raton-lesion/abogado-accidentes-auto/juicio/
https://www.kogan-disalvo.com/es/boca-raton-lesion/abogado-accidentes-auto/mandando-mensajes-mientras-conduciendo/responsabilidad/
https://www.kogan-disalvo.com/es/boca-raton-lesion/abogado-accidentes-auto/medico-tratamiento/
https://www.kogan-disalvo.com/es/boca-raton-lesion/abogado-accidentes-auto/plazo-de-prescripcion/
https://www.kogan-disalvo.com/es/boca-raton-lesion/abogado-accidentes-auto/proteccion-por-lesiones-personales-cobertura/
https://www.kogan-disalvo.com/es/boca-raton-lesion/abogado-accidentes-auto/testigos-expertos/

The folder https://www.kogan-disalvo.com/es/boca-raton-lesion/abogado-accidentes-auto/ does not exist. The car accident page for Boca Raton, should be this: https://www.kogan-disalvo.com/es/boca-raton-lesion/abogado-accidentes-coche/. There's also this URL: https://www.kogan-disalvo.com/es/boca-raton-lesion/abogado-accidentes-carro/, which is a 404.

Another example:

https://www.kogan-disalvo.com/es/boca-raton-lesion/abogado-accidentes-peaton/
https://www.kogan-disalvo.com/es/boca-raton-lesion/abogado-accidentes-peaton/corredores/
https://www.kogan-disalvo.com/es/boca-raton-lesion/abogado-accidentes-peaton/leyes/
https://www.kogan-disalvo.com/es/boca-raton-lesion/abogado-accidentes-peatonales/determinacion-responsabilidad/
https://www.kogan-disalvo.com/es/boca-raton-lesion/abogado-accidentes-peatones/danos/
https://www.kogan-disalvo.com/es/boca-raton-lesion/abogado-accidentes-peatones/escenarios/

The working URL is https://www.kogan-disalvo.com/es/boca-raton-lesion/abogado-accidentes-peaton/ but at some point, it translated that folder differently, causing a URL that is a 404.

More such examples can be found in this sheet:

https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1uAHDr_2tGa-Vo3Y0uE8b5KgE6c2puOqctzJnEfvzD7Y/edit?usp=sharing

So, we need the correct URLs to remain the same when they are parents to child pages.

title: Reporting Call Prep
description:
This task must be fully updated by EOD Monday before the reporting call.

This document serves as:
  • Internal alignment between AM, CM, and SEO
  • Source of truth for call structure
  • Risk detection tool
  • Talking point preparation
No one should walk into the reporting call unprepared.

This prep aligns directly with the live call agenda.

AM should use this document to guide the call in real time.

No one should enter the call without this fully filled out.

SECTION 1: OPENING FRAME (AM)


What tone are we setting?

Client temperature this month:
 ☐ Strong
 ☐ Neutral
 ☐ Slightly concerned
 ☐ Frustrated

Notes from weekly pulse:


Any relationship items to acknowledge at opening?

Opening focus reminder:
What do we want the client to feel by minute 3?

SECTION 2: PERFORMANCE OVERVIEW (CM SECTION)


@LeadSEO please provide by Wednesday 12 PM:
  1. How is performance this month?
  2. Campaign health status: Green / Yellow / Red
  3. Areas of concern
  4. Anything stand out? Wins or things we need to be aware of?
  5. Do you need anything from the client?
  6. What is our main focus next month?
AM must review and confirm alignment with CM before call.

Risk Flag:
 ☐ No performance concerns
 ☐ Minor fluctuation
 ☐ Underperformance risk

If risk exists:
  • Has CM been aligned?
  • Has AM Lead been notified?
No surprises allowed on call.

SECTION 3: PERFORMANCE Q&A PREP


List anticipated performance questions:




Confirm:

☐ CM prepared for these
☐ Messaging aligned
☐ Time boundary plan set

SECTION 4: WORK COMPLETED (AM SECTION)


This mirrors the “Campaign Execution” portion of the call.

List in client-friendly language:
  • SEO deliverables completed:

  • Content published:

  • Links built:

  • Technical fixes:

  • Dev completed:

  • Wins (Sentinel, visibility gains, authority growth):

No internal jargon.

SECTION 5: WORK IN PROGRESS (AM)


  • Active SEO initiatives:

  • Content pipeline:

  • Technical initiatives:

  • Dev in progress:

  • Strategic pushes:

Must clearly show momentum.

SECTION 6: TEAM NEEDS (AM)


Do we need anything from the client?
  • Approvals:

  • Content:

  • Access:

  • Decisions:


If nothing needed, state clearly: None at this time.

SECTION 7: MONTH AHEAD VISION (AM)


This section drives confidence.

Answer clearly:
  • What is our main focus next month?
  • What are we monitoring closely?
  • What would success look like by next reporting call?

SECTION 8: FINAL Q&A PREP (AM)


Non-performance questions the client may raise:


Plan response or follow-up pathway.

Confirm:

☐ If unsure, defer and follow up
☐ CM will not re-enter unless pre-discussed

SECTION 9: ESCALATION CHECK


Before call, confirm:

☐ SEO updates received
☐ Dev updates confirmed
☐ Any blockers escalated
☐ AM Lead notified if risk exists
☐ CM aligned on any sensitive topics

If any box cannot be checked, escalate before call.

SECTION 10: CALL CONTROL REMINDERS (AM)


  • Set agenda at beginning
  • Protect time at minute 20
  • Transition clearly
  • Do not guess
  • Close confidently

title: Reporting Call Prep
description:
Client temperature this month:
 ☐ Strong
 ☐ Neutral
☐ Slightly concerned
☐ Frustrated

Notes from weekly pulse:

  • Grade for Google Search Console Data Sources: 5/5. Reasons for a Full Score:

    • Comprehensiveness: Key SEO metrics are present, including both aggregate and keyword-level breakdowns.
    • Freshness: Data covers daily performance, showing granular and up-to-date insights.
    • Coverage: Both overall totals and individual keyword performance (including brand and non-brand terms) are shown.
    • Field Status: All relevant fields are marked as "ok," indicating healthy, functioning data connections.
    • Actionability: The available data provides actionable insights for ranking, visibility, and optimization.
What’s Present:

  • Complete keyword table (positions, impressions, clicks, CTR for each term).
  • Performance over date intervals (with health status).
  • Brand and competitive keywords both included.
  • GBP
  •  
    Strengths

    • Excellent Reputation
      • The firm maintains a 5-star average rating from a significant volume of reviews.
      • Recent client feedback consistently highlights positive experiences, professionalism, and excellent results.
      • This high rating and strong sentiment are powerful signals for Google’s local ranking algorithm.
    • High Visibility & Engagement
      • 4,958 total impressions in November 2025 indicate the listing is being viewed often in relevant local searches.
      • 439 total interactions (calls, website clicks, and other engagement actions) show that users aren’t just seeing the listing—they’re taking meaningful actions.
    • Conversion-Driven Behavior
      • 109 call clicks and 140 website visits directly from Google Business Profile in a single month reflect great interest and new client inquiries.
      • Consistent engagement means the GBP is successfully driving real-world business leads.
    • Wide Coverage
      • The client’s listing appears in both Google Search and Google Maps, across mobile and desktop platforms, ensuring all user types can find and interact with the firm.
    • Continuous Community Activity
      • Regular positive reviews and community buzz reinforce trust, which boosts both rankings and new business opportunities.

Any relationship items to acknowledge at opening?

13 reinstated review across all locations

title: Monitor GBP Photos if still marked as pending
Include Sitemap In footer of Client websites
description:
Hi, this is inline w/ a group of tasks we have from a sheet. Here is the link for reference: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1qPfUUC1J2r1s7Ot8Y1PmIcje6WpyMszDhxHGz4TxDTk/edit?gid=0#gid=0

SEO added more info column D. 

I have included the task info here:
--- 
Hey Team!

To enhance our technical SEO baseline, improve crawl efficiency, and ensure consistent signaling across all client properties, It would be great to come to an agreement to outlining a new, standardized protocol for crawl management.

My suggestion is as follows:

Implement the following three directives on all client websites as part of our standard build and maintenance process.

1. XML Sitemap in robots.txt (Primary Directive)

This is the most effective way to inform search engines of all indexable URLs.

Action: Ensure the robots.txt file for every client domain contains a Sitemap directive pointing to the full URL of the XML sitemap.

Implementation Example:
User-agent: 
Sitemap: https://www.clientdomain.com/sitemap.xml

The "Why": This provides a clear, direct path for Google to find and crawl our most important pages, ensuring faster discovery and indexing of new content.

2. User-Facing HTML Sitemap (Footer)

This is for user experience and secondary crawl pathing.

Action: All client websites must have a user-facing HTML sitemap.

Implementation: This sitemap must be linked in the footer of every page on the site.

The "Why": This provides a clear navigation path for users, improves site accessibility, and   acts as a secondary crawl path for search engines to discover deep pages.

Strategic Crawl & Indexation Control

This is critical. We must be precise in telling crawlers what to crawl and what to index.

A. To Control Crawling (Using robots.txt)

We must Disallow crawlers from accessing low-value, non-SEO pages to conserve crawl budget.

Action: Audit and update the robots.txt file to Disallow non-public or utility sections of the site.
Implementation Examples:
Disallow: /wp-admin/

The WP sitemap page  plugin in perfect to use as a plugin for now

Regarding providing a robot.txt:

They differ per client especially regarding disallows but here is a comprehensive one that can be edited according to client needs especially regarding disallows:

User-agent: *

# --- Core WordPress File Disallows ---
# Block all core administrative and include folders.
Disallow: /wp-admin/
Disallow: /wp-includes/

# --- Crawl Budget Optimization ---
# Block non-media plugin and theme files from being crawled.
Disallow: /wp-content/plugins/
Disallow: /wp-content/themes/

# Block internal search result pages, which are low-quality, duplicate content.
Disallow: /?s=
Disallow: /search/

# Block other non-public WordPress files and pages.
Disallow: /wp-login.php
Disallow: /readme.html
Disallow: /trackback/
Disallow: /xmlrpc.php

# --- Allow Directives for Critical Files ---
# Explicitly ALLOW critical AJAX and asset files that Google
# may need for rendering, even if their parent folder is disallowed.
Allow: /wp-admin/admin-ajax.php
Allow: /wp-content/uploads/

# --- Sitemap Directive ---
# This is the primary directive to tell all search engines where our XML sitemap is located.
# [IMPORTANT: Replace with the client's actual sitemap URL]
Sitemap: https://www.clientdomain.com/sitemap_index.xml

Also, just an example of placement. Sitemap listed at the very bottom of the footer on the left followed by privacy policy etc.

* In the footer we will use the page /sitemap/ and not the XML sitemap.  The XML sitemap is for Google & the robots.txt.


you can use this as robots.txt:
 
User-agent: *

# --- Core WordPress File Disallows ---
# Block all core administrative and include folders.
Disallow: /wp-admin/
Disallow: /wp-includes/

# --- Crawl Budget Optimization ---
# Block non-media plugin and theme files from being crawled.
Disallow: /wp-content/plugins/
Disallow: /wp-content/themes/

# Block internal search result pages, which are low-quality, duplicate content.
Disallow: /?s=
Disallow: /search/

# Block other non-public WordPress files and pages.
Disallow: /wp-login.php
Disallow: /readme.html
Disallow: /trackback/
Disallow: /xmlrpc.php

# --- Allow Directives for Critical Files ---
# Explicitly ALLOW critical AJAX and asset files that Google 
# may need for rendering, even if their parent folder is disallowed.
Allow: /wp-admin/admin-ajax.php
Allow: /wp-content/uploads/

# --- Sitemap Directive ---
# This is the primary directive to tell all search engines where our XML sitemap is located.
# [IMPORTANT: Replace with the client's actual sitemap URL]
Sitemap: https://www.goarco.com/sitemap_index.xml

Thanks much!

title: Location Page Changes And ToC Changes
assignees: Den, Evgenii Gusarovskii, Jordan, Mary De Guzman, Predrag
description:
Hi

I need help with 2 things:

1. We need the ToC on pages to have a toggle and for it to be closed by default. This is for all pages that use it.


2. On Location pages and the pages under them, I need a button that says Call Us, right under the page title on the header image. Some location case type pages already have such a button that leads to the contact us page. That can be repurposed for the phone number.

Thanks much.

title: Redirect loop
description:
Hello.

We have this URL with double slash: https://www.kogan-disalvo.com//boca-raton-injury/truck-accident-lawyer/what-evidence-do-i-need-to-prove-my-truck-accident-case-in-florida/ that redirect loops.

I found the page and removed the extra slash, so it was : https://www.kogan-disalvo.com/boca-raton-injury/truck-accident-lawyer/what-evidence-do-i-need-to-prove-my-truck-accident-case-in-florida/

But it still loops. So I deleted the page and created a new one, same URL and everything, and it does the same. 

There are no redirects for that page.
I tried not using the Custom Link functionality but it looks weird without it, even with the correct parent set.

It links from this page: https://www.kogan-disalvo.com/boca-raton-injury/truck-accident-lawyer/ and probably the sitemap.

Can you guys take a look?

title: NAP & HTML Cleanup Required Across Website
description:
Hi Clayton,

This is inline w/ the ticket that Savana sent yesterday about Kogan's NAP & HTML Cleanup Required Across Website. Seeking help if there is still something that needs to be done for it or we may need from the account manager.

--- I have included the email info below: ---
Hi Team,
 
I’ve completed an evaluation of our hardcoded NAP numbers and phone links across the site, and there are several issues that require immediate cleanup.
  1. NAP inconsistencies: The phone numbers currently hardcoded in some location silos do not match the correct numbers for those locations.
  2. Incorrect HTML structure: On some pages, the HTML for phone links is broken or improperly formatted, which could affect both usability and SEO.
  1. Header/Footer/Contact pages: These sitewide numbers need to be using the 800 number to connect with the Website Pool – Header/Footer
 
We need a full cleanup of these elements with modifications as we continue to grow. I’ve attached the correct mapping of Location Silos → NAP (hardcoded) → Website Pool for reference. Please ensure that the numbers are hardcoded correctly per their respective silos.
 
I will handle updating and editing the website pools on the CallRail side, but please confirm that the CallRail JavaScript is implemented sitewide to ensure tracking works properly across all pages.
 
Action Items for SEO/Web Team:
  • Review the attached sheet for correct NAP assignments. (the "attached sheet" is the table below)
  • Clean up all hardcoded numbers and ensure they match their location silo.
  • Fix HTML structure issues on affected pages.
  • Confirm CallRail JavaScript is sitewide.
  •  
Let’s aim to get this resolved quickly to avoid SEO and tracking discrepancies.



Thanks much.

CC

title: Share Content on GBP
description:
https://www.kogan-disalvo.com/medical-malpractice/is-there-a-difference-between-misdiagnosis-and-delayed-diagnosis/

In this article we discuss the difference between "misdiagnosis" and "delayed diagnosis" in the context of medical malpractice. A misdiagnosis occurs when a healthcare provider incorrectly identifies a patient's condition, potentially leading to improper or harmful treatment. In contrast, a delayed diagnosis involves a healthcare provider taking too long to diagnose a condition, which may cause the condition to worsen due to lack of timely treatment. Both situations can result in harm to the patient and may be grounds for a medical malpractice claim. We likely also provide information on legal considerations and options for patients who have experienced either of these issues. If you need legal guidance, don't hesitate to contact our experienced lawyers. Kogan & DiSalvo Personal Injury Lawyers are always here for your needs!
#MedicalMalpractice #Misdiagnosis #DelayedDiagnosis #LegalRepresentation #Kogan&DiSalvoPersonalInjuryLawyers

At Kogan & DiSalvo Personal Injury Lawyers, we explain that in Florida, patients may have grounds to file a lawsuit for delays in medical diagnosis if those delays amount to medical malpractice. Our article discusses how such delays can occur, the legal requirements for bringing a claim, and the potential obstacles involved in pursuing these cases. We highlight the necessity of proving negligence, often through expert testimony, and explain the types of damages that victims may be entitled to seek. Our goal is to provide individuals with a clear understanding of their legal rights and options when facing harm due to diagnostic delays.

title: June Scope of Work Report
assignees: Chris Traynor, Clayton Coombs, Dorin Gherman, Will Pettet
description:
I've reached out to Christine for additional clarification regarding what she wants from the June Scope of Work report. This was her reply:

To provide more context on what we’re looking for in the SEO scope of work reports:
 
Work Done on the Website:
 
Detailed information on on-page SEO activities, including content updates, meta tags optimization, and any structural changes.
Insights into technical SEO improvements, such as site speed optimizations, fixing crawl errors, and mobile usability enhancements.
 
Pages and Backlinks Created:
 
A list of new pages created, along with their respective content and targeted keywords.
Information on the backlinks acquired, including the sources, types of links (e.g., guest posts, directories), and their impact on our SEO performance.
 
Performance Metrics and Cadence:
 
Overview of key performance metrics such as organic traffic, keyword rankings, and conversion rates.
A timeline or cadence of the work performed throughout the month to understand the consistency and frequency of SEO activities.
 
Future Strategy and Recommendations:
 
Any recommendations for ongoing and future SEO efforts based on the current month’s performance.
Upcoming plans or strategies to address any identified gaps or opportunities.

  , I think we should table this until Will and Clayton are back so I can discuss this request with them. Just dropping this here for context and clarity.

Ty!

title: Website Audit Kogan & DiSalvo
assignees: Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, Den, Jordan, Keith Bruss, Predrag
description:
Website Audit

  • Look into the theme.
  • Look into how the pages are built
  • Look into Custom Post Types
  • Look into Plugins
  • Look into performance optimization and caching strategy
  • Look into forms
  • Look into 3rd party integrations
  • Look for previous agency artifacts
  • Remove previous agency emails, etc
 

Theme:
Theme Overview:
  • Identify the current theme.
  • Check if it's a custom or premium theme.
  • Ensure the theme is up-to-date.
Child Theme:
  • Check if there's a child theme for customizations.
  • Evaluate the purpose and functionality of the child theme.
Theme Customizations:
  • Review theme settings and options.
  • Check for custom CSS or JavaScript modifications.
 

Page Structure:
Page Builder:
  • Identify if a page builder is used (e.g., Elementor, Beaver Builder).
  • Check for custom templates or layouts.
Custom Page Templates:
  • Look for any custom page templates.
  • Ensure compatibility with the current theme.
 

Custom Post Types:
Identify Custom Post Types:
  • List and review custom post types.
  • Check for relevance and proper integration.
Custom Taxonomies:
  • Examine custom taxonomies and their usage.
  • Verify consistency and accuracy.
 

Plugins:
Installed Plugins:
  • List all installed plugins.
  • Check for outdated or unsupported plugins.
Plugin License:
  • Review plugin & identify premium plugins.
  • Ensure licenses are up to date.
Plugin Configuration:
  • Review plugin settings and configurations.
  • Ensure proper integration with the site.
 

Performance Optimization:
Performance Plugins:
  • Identify and review performance plugins (e.g., caching, minification).
  • Evaluate their effectiveness.
Caching Strategy:
  • Analyze the caching strategy in place.
  • Check for proper configuration and optimization.
 

  1. Initial Analysis

    • Conduct a website speed test using tools like Google PageSpeed Insights, GTmetrix, or Pingdom.
    • Analyze current load times and performance metrics.

  2. Server and Hosting Optimization

    • Upgrade to a faster hosting plan or switch to a high-performance hosting provider.
    • Enable HTTP/2 for faster loading of web pages.
    • Use a content delivery network (CDN) to distribute content globally and reduce latency.

  3. WordPress Configuration

    • Update WordPress core, themes, and plugins to the latest versions.
    • Remove any unused or unnecessary plugins and themes.
    • Limit post revisions and disable autosave frequency to reduce database bloat.

  4. Caching

    • Install and configure a caching plugin (e.g., W3 Total Cache, WP Super Cache, or WP Rocket).
    • Enable browser caching to store static files on users’ devices.
    • Implement server-side caching (e.g., Varnish or Nginx FastCGI cache).

  5. Database Optimization

    • Optimize the database using plugins like WP-Optimize or WP Sweep.
    • Regularly clean up the database by removing spam comments, post revisions, and transients.
    • Implement database caching to reduce the load on the database.

  6. Image Optimization

    • Compress images using tools like TinyPNG or WP Smush.
    • Use next-gen image formats like WebP for better compression.
    • Implement lazy loading for images and iframes to load them only when they are in the viewport.
    • Serve scaled images that are properly sized for their container.

  7. Minification and Concatenation

    • Minify CSS, JavaScript, and HTML files to reduce their size.
    • Combine multiple CSS and JavaScript files to reduce HTTP requests.

  8. Content Delivery Network (CDN)

    • Configure a CDN service (e.g., Cloudflare, StackPath, or KeyCDN) to serve static assets.
    • Ensure CDN is set up to serve images, CSS, JavaScript, and other static resources.

  9. Reduce Server Requests

    • Limit the use of external scripts and resources.
    • Use inline CSS for above-the-fold content to reduce render-blocking resources.
    • Defer non-essential JavaScript to prevent it from blocking page load.

  10. Optimize Fonts

    • Use system fonts or web-safe fonts to reduce load times.
    • Host web fonts locally to eliminate third-party requests.
    • Use font-display: swap in CSS to ensure text remains visible during web font load.

  11. Enable Gzip Compression

    • Enable Gzip or Brotli compression on the server to reduce the size of files sent to the browser.

  12. Code and Script Optimization

    • Remove unnecessary CSS and JavaScript.
    • Asynchronously load JavaScript to improve page rendering times.
    • Use modern JavaScript libraries and frameworks that are optimized for performance.

  13. Monitoring and Maintenance

    • Set up monitoring tools to continuously track website performance.
    • Regularly check for broken links and fix them to avoid unnecessary server requests.
    • Schedule periodic performance audits to identify and address new issues.

  14. Additional Tweaks

    • Implement Accelerated Mobile Pages (AMP) for faster mobile performance.
    • Use a lightweight theme optimized for speed.
    • Reduce redirects and eliminate unnecessary ones.
    • Optimize the delivery of critical CSS to ensure faster rendering.
 

Forms:
  1. Form Plugins:
    • Identify form plugins in use (e.g., Contact Form 7, Gravity Forms).
    • Check form configurations and notifications.
    • Add keith@clixsy.com as a BCC to form submissions


SMTP Server:
  1. WP SMTP Mail:
    • Identify SMTP server used
    • Ensure client's intake dept can receive emails


3rd Party Integrations:
Integration Plugins:
  • List and review plugins for third-party integrations.
  • Ensure they are functioning correctly.
API Integrations:
  • Identify any custom API integrations.
  • Verify the status and functionality of each integration.
Video Embeds:
  • Identify any video embeds and determine ownership of accounts
 

Cleanup:
Agency Artifacts:
  • Remove previous agency branding, links, or mentions.
  • Remove previous agency from form notifications.
  • Replace any placeholder or demo content.
User Accounts:
  • Remove or update user accounts associated with the previous agency.
  • Ensure that only relevant personnel have access.
Emails and Notifications:
  • Update contact emails and notification settings.
  • Ensure the website is sending emails correctly.
 

Security:
Security Plugins:
  • Identify and review security plugins.
  • Ensure they are configured properly.
User Permissions:
  • Review user roles and permissions.
  • Update and secure access for all users.
 

Documentation:
  1. Documentation Review:
    • Check for existing documentation on site structure, customizations, and configurations.
    • Update or create documentation for future reference.
 

Testing:
Cross-Browser Testing:
  • Test the website on different browsers to ensure compatibility.
Mobile Responsiveness:
  • Verify that the website is responsive on various devices.
Functionality Testing:
  • Test critical functionalities, such as forms, e-commerce, and interactive elements.
Backup:
  • Perform a backup before making any significant changes.
By systematically addressing these points, you can ensure a smooth transition when taking over a client's website and optimize its performance and functionality.

title: Send Welcome Email
list_title: ONBOARDING › Pre-Discovery ADMIN / ACCOUNT MANAGER / PM
description:
Dear XXXXX (Client Contact),

On behalf of the entire Clixsy staff, I’d like to take this opportunity to welcome you as a new client. We are thrilled to have you with us.

I am also happy to say that I will be your primary point of contact, and please get in touch at any time with your questions, comments and feedback.

NEW CLIENT INTAKE FORM
 
I have linked you to our New Client Intake Form, below. Can you, or a designated individual, please fill it in and submit at your earliest convenience?
 
This New Client Intake Form forms the basis for our Discovery and initial kick-off meeting in which we’ll get together to begin working together and establishing roles, responsibilities and expectations. 
 
Please ensure you thoroughly fill in what you can, and if you are not able to fill something in, just let us know and we’ll work with you to either obtain the information or determine whether it’s a priority at this time. 
 
There are sections with requests for technical information like usernames and passwords for your online assets. Please try to be as accurate as possible with these so we can get in and audit your website/social/advertising accounts without having to return to you with further requests. This may slow the process up and cost us valuable time.
 
 Don’t worry if you can’t, or are unwilling to disclose all the information we have requested as the main goal here is to have at hand any resources we’ll need to work efficiently and to ensure that we organize and maintain current/accurate information for you so that you’ll never have to say “I don’t know” when a vendor or internal partner/employee asks “who has the login to XYZ”. Not to mention any information you can supply will help get the best returns we can from your investment with us. Just try and be as accurate and as thorough as you can. We will cover everything in more depth at your Discovery Meeting. 
 
http://www.clixsy.com/intake/

DISCOVERY MEETING
 
As soon as we have received and processed your New Client Intake Form, I will reach out to find a time to hold your Discovery Meeting. This will be held online via a Zoom call. 

 In the Discovery Meeting we will answer any questions you may have and talk about who on our team addresses the various roles and responsibilities, what next steps will be and establish our “battle rhythm” as our teams determine how best to work in unison. We will use the New Client Intake Form to discuss things like, your business and campaign goals, KPIs, customer journeys through your digital assets, your competitors etc. We will brainstorm the best methods to reach your goals and agree a ‘Definition of DONE’, for each stage of the process. 
 
We will also discuss any legal advertising constraints there may be in your industry and set boundaries based on what your company culture is comfortable with, and what is appropriate in the market. 
 
This should give us all the information we need to get started our work in increasing your market share and reaching your business goals. 
 
Once again, thank you for entrusting Clixsy with your business. Together we'll do our best to get you as big an ROI and slice of the market share as we can, as quickly as possible,
 
 
 Best regards,
 
 
 
 R. Corey Vandenberg 
President 
Corey@clixsy.com 
801-645-6466

title: Finalise Discovery Meeting (DM) agenda
list_title: ONBOARDING › Pre-Discovery ADMIN / ACCOUNT MANAGER / PM
description:
After the Client submits the DQ, you should outline the agenda of the Discovery Meeting, utilizing the form fields below.

Check and Email to Client. Should be typical but add Job No. and Date/Time of Discovery Meeting.


Discovery Meeting Date will be set.


Discovery Meeting Time.


Discovery Meeting Agenda



Link to Discovery Meeting Agenda... 


For the Customer

Indepth study of Discovery Questionnaire paying particular attention to:Typical Buyer Persona. See how to easily create your Typical Buyer Persona with our online slideshow.

Typical Customer Journey. Here’s great article on how you can easily and quickly create your typical Customer Journey.

Your business/campaign goals and how to measure them using KPIs. Here’s a great article on how to translate your business goals into measureable KPIs.How to maximise the ROI from your Facebook Ads Campaign.

Common objections to sales in your industry.Your competitors, what they do well and what they could do better. How we can win in the market.Any legal constraints to advertising in your industry.

Brainstorming campaign design to keep our campaign integrated with your other marketing efforts. Here’s an article which does a good job of explaining what Integrated Marketing is, and here’s another one which shows some great examples.

Please remember to bring logos/images/other advertising materials with you to the Discovery Meeting.

For us

Upsell additional services

Take receipt of all Client logos, images, further materials

Scope of Works (SoW) finalised

New Price & Payment Terms agreed

Get Final Client Contract written up while Client is in office

Final Client Contract (FCC) signed

ASK FOR REFERRALS

description:
including...


Confirmation of Discovery Meeting Date
...with DM Time
...and DM Agenda
Map & Directions to our office (if they can attend our premises)

Also remind them of the importance of filling out the Discovery Questionnaire before the DM if they haven't already submitted it.

Subject: OUR REF: [Project No.] Clixsy - Discovery Meeting Confirmation

Hi [Client Name],

further to our phone call/your email, [DELETE AS APPROPRIATE] I am happy to confirm the following date and time for your Discovery Meeting at our/your office/by phone/by Skype: [DELETE AS APPROPRIATE]

Date: [DATE]
Time: [Time]

Please find attached a map with directions to our office from yours/Zoom Link and we look forward to progressing with your project!

[EITHER]

Please can you fill in your Discovery Questionnaire and email it back to me as soon as you can so I can properly finalise our Discovery Meeting Agenda? It was attached in your Welcome Email sent on [WELCOME EMAIL DATE/TIME] and we have attached another copy to this email.

[OR]

Thank you for sending your Discovery Questionnaire back to us so promptly. We have attached the Discovery Meeting Agenda you can have an early look. This will allow us to focus on the important parts of your campaign at the meeting.

Please find attached a map with directions to our office from yours and we look forward to progressing with your project!

Best regards,

for Clixsy

title: Prepare Discovery Meeting Agenda
description:
DISCOVERY MEETING INTERNAL CHECKLIST


Have you discussed the Client's Business/Campaign Goals? One goal per campaign.

Have you built a Typical Customer Journey for the Client's business?

Have you built a Typical Buyer Persona for each campaign?

Have you decided on Campaign KPIs to measure our success?

Have you agreed on Monthly KPI Targets?

Have you set Campaign Budgets?

Have you spoken about common objections to sales the Client has experienced?

Have you done a SWOT analysis on each of the Client's Top 3 competitors?

Has Client intimated any legal constraints to advertising?

Have you discussed Integrated Marketing and looked at Client's past and current advertising campaigns?

Has the Client brought images/logos etc.?

Have you brainstormed ideas for creatives/messages/slogans/copy etc.?

Have you suggested any further services that could be beneficial to the Client?

If the CPC has changed have you transferred changes to Final Client Contract (FCC)?

Have you agreed the new Price & Payment Terms?

Have you written up the Final Client Contract (FCC) while Client is still in the office?

Have you got the Final Client Contract (FCC) signed?

Have you discussed incentives/discounts for referrals?

Have you asked for referrals? Add the on the next sheet.

title: Email Discovery Meeting Agenda to client
Discovery Meeting
description:
Follow the Discovery Meeting Agenda prepared before.

Remember to ask for referrals!

Ask if they know anyone else you should talk to who is facing similar issues or runs a similar type of business.  Record any referred leads in the form field below.

Transfer all Referrals to CRM for follow up.

Referrals Contact Sheet uploaded here.

title: Upload signed Final Client Contract (FCC)
description:
Answer any questions and have the client sign the FCC during the Discovery Meeting.

Once again, record a copy of the signed FCC in the form field below.

title: Email copy of signed FCC to client
Write up meeting notes
description:
Write up internal notes for transfer to Scope of Works (SoW).

Add this to CRM as things tend to get lost in email (again, this can be set up to happen automatically with the form field).

Include the agenda but put specific action points for team members to start working on.

...and email copy to Client.

title: Send hand-written Thank-You letter
description: This is a great touch that many companies have found success with; you need to send a handwritten thank-you note to each client, hand-signed.

title: Household accounts and link to other relationships
description: Also in your CRM, link interactions with this client to any other clients in your CRM. Use the form field below to note down previous roles/companies this client has worked at and see if you can use this to generate some additional business now or in the future.

title: Upload all completed Docs to Client Folder
Handover Meeting with SEO Team
description:
ONLY TO BE DONE WHEN INVOICE IS PAID (Or First-stage payment received)

Zip SoW and all completed docs from last 2 sections.

Email to Project Manager.

Handover Meeting.

Assign to an SEO manager to analyze data from prior steps and write report with conclusions with actionable insights.

Here's an example from Munley: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1bMayLSFjITUaoL44sPcWJLnYc9nAakwkwoAnM6f6egY/edit?usp=sharing

Skip to the SEO review section for example relevant to this.

title: Establish LSA Access
description:
1. Pull the CID from the onboarding form (found under "CLIENT WORKBOOK" door on the project's front page, then the "Project Details" tab. Should be ROW 28.)
  2. Send a management request using the Google Ads interface. TUTORIAL
  3. Verify that the management request was sent. TUTORIAL
  4. Send the client an email advising them to look out for the management request. Provide them with THIS TUTORIAL to reference should they encounter any issues.
  5. Check this off once the client has accepted the management request
